summary: Excellent early soundboard capture of a two‑song set featuring a blistering Alligator and the debut Lovelight.
review: The recording, sourced from a 1967 soundboard, is acceptably clear for its era and lets the raw power of the performance shine through. Both "Alligator" and the first (or early) "Turn On Your Lovelight" are hailed as standout moments, with Pigpen’s vocals and Jerry’s guitar work highlighted throughout. Though only two songs were played, the energy is high and the tape remains a prized glimpse of the young Dead.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic reviews praise both songs and note decent soundboard quality; the show is deemed exceptional despite its brevity, leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound quality described as acceptably workable/clear for a 1967 capture; no major drop‑outs or mix issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A very short two‑song set (Alligator and Lovelight) that delivers high energy and historic significance; reviewers note the rarity of hearing both early gems together.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for tearing it up, digging deep, and delivering powerful guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for a rich, full guitar tone on Alligator.
